# JWT Arsenal

> 100% client-side JWT exploitation toolkit for pentesters, CTF players and bug bounty hunters. Forge, inspect and exploit JWT vulnerabilities directly in your browser - no server, no data leaves your machine.

URL: https://jwtarsenal.com
Source: https://github.com/HiitCat/JWT-Arsenal
License: MIT

## Exploit Tools

Each tool runs entirely client-side using the Web Crypto API and the jose library.

- [JWT Inspector](https://jwtarsenal.com/inspect): Decode and inspect any JWT token - header, payload, signature - with color-coded output.
- [Unverified Signature](https://jwtarsenal.com/exploit/unverified-signature): Test whether a server validates JWT signatures or merely decodes the payload.
- [Algorithm None](https://jwtarsenal.com/exploit/alg-none): Forge unsigned JWT tokens by setting alg to "none". Generates all case variants (none, None, NONE, nOnE).
- [Algorithm Confusion](https://jwtarsenal.com/exploit/algorithm-confusion): Switch alg from RS256 to HS256 and sign with the RSA public key as HMAC secret.
- [KID Injection](https://jwtarsenal.com/exploit/kid-injection): Inject path traversal, SQL, or arbitrary values into the kid header parameter.
- [JWK Injection](https://jwtarsenal.com/exploit/jwk-injection): Embed an attacker-controlled RSA public key in the JWT header (jwk field).
- [JKU Injection](https://jwtarsenal.com/exploit/jku-injection): Replace jku with an attacker-controlled JWKS endpoint URL, includes self-hosted JWKS.
- [Public Key Recovery](https://jwtarsenal.com/exploit/public-key-recovery): Recover the RSA public key from two signed JWTs via GCD, then chain to algorithm confusion.

## Knowledge Base

In-depth technical articles on each attack technique.

- [JWT Structure & Internals](https://jwtarsenal.com/knowledge-base/jwt-structure): Header fields, payload claims, base64url encoding, JOSE standards.
- [Unverified Signature](https://jwtarsenal.com/knowledge-base/unverified-signature): How and why servers skip signature verification.
- [Algorithm None](https://jwtarsenal.com/knowledge-base/alg-none): RFC 7518 loophole, case variation bypasses, affected libraries (2015 disclosure).
- [Algorithm Confusion](https://jwtarsenal.com/knowledge-base/algorithm-confusion): RS256→HS256 mechanics, key format details, ES256 variant, vulnerable code patterns.
- [KID Injection](https://jwtarsenal.com/knowledge-base/kid-injection): Path traversal, SQL injection, SSRF via the kid header.
- [JWK Injection](https://jwtarsenal.com/knowledge-base/jwk-injection): Embedding attacker keys in the token header.
- [JKU Injection](https://jwtarsenal.com/knowledge-base/jku-injection): JWKS endpoint hijacking and bypass techniques.
- [Public Key Recovery](https://jwtarsenal.com/knowledge-base/public-key-recovery): GCD-based RSA key recovery from two signatures.

## CLI Cheatsheet

- [CLI Cheatsheet](https://jwtarsenal.com/cheatsheet): Commands for jwt_tool, hashcat (mode 16500), and rsa_sign2n covering all attack techniques.

## Quick Reference

Common questions answered:

- How to exploit alg:none → https://jwtarsenal.com/exploit/alg-none
- How to perform algorithm confusion RS256→HS256 → https://jwtarsenal.com/exploit/algorithm-confusion
- How to recover RSA public key from two JWTs → https://jwtarsenal.com/exploit/public-key-recovery
- JWT security testing cheatsheet → https://jwtarsenal.com/cheatsheet
- All JWT attack techniques explained → https://jwtarsenal.com/knowledge-base
